title: Node File Services, Object Storage, and CDN Architecture · Contract Lens: Cost Failure Model
summary: Predict unknown input, typed state, command, stream, promise, storage, cache, security, cost, and recovery outcomes before reviewing Node storage-architecture decision record; use a typed control plane separated from bounded direct or streamed data planes. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cost failure model increment in Node storage-architecture decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, model storage, request, transfer, retrieval, invalidation, compute, and operational cost together with regional and dependency failures.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, assign creation, quarantine, scan, publish, replace, archive, restore, revoke, and delete ownership with terminal states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Cost Failure Model · Theory Cost Failure Model
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 9818 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to select a typed control and data plane from trust, transformation, and volume evidence; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then change region, retrieval, or transfer cost and defend the revised budget action. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, model storage, request, transfer, retrieval, invalidation, compute, and operational cost together with regional and dependency failures.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Cost Failure Model contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Build Cost Failure Model evidence · Theory Cost Failure Model
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-cost-failure-model-wp7b5m" that uses case 7489 to select a typed control and data plane from trust, transformation, and volume evidence. Concept boundary: contrast one accepted path, one near miss, and the claim that still needs transfer evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
